blocking_issues.title: No pre-deploy PostgreSQL version check
blocking_issues.detail: Migration uses ADD COLUMN with DEFAULT, which is an online operation on PG11+ but causes a full table rewrite (and lock) on PG<11. No automated check exists to verify the server version before running the migration.
blocking_issues.resolution: Add version check to migration script or pre-deploy checklist. Recommended: `DO $$ BEGIN IF current_setting('server_version_num')::int < 110000 THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'PostgreSQL 11+ required'; END IF; END $$;`

blocking_issues.title: Redis cache not invalidated after retry_count update
blocking_issues.detail: repository.go increments retry_count in PostgreSQL but does not invalidate or update the Redis cache entry. API reads from cache first, so users see stale retry_count for up to 5 minutes (cache TTL).
blocking_issues.resolution: Add `redis.Del(ctx, fmt.Sprintf("payment:%s", id))` after the UPDATE query in repository.go. Alternatively, update the cache entry inline.

warnings.title: Retry worker uses N+1 query pattern
warnings.detail: Worker reads retry_count individually for each payment in the batch. At 200 payments per batch, this adds ~200 queries per 30s cycle. Will degrade linearly.
warnings.recommendation: Refactor to batch query before next release. Not blocking because current batch sizes are small enough.

warnings.title: No alert threshold on retry worker cycle duration
warnings.detail: Worker has a 30s ticker but no alert if a cycle exceeds 30s. Ticks would accumulate silently.

warnings.title: Retry cap has no configurable override
warnings.detail: Retry cap is hardcoded to 3 in service.go. If the cap needs adjustment in production, it requires a code deploy.
warnings.recommendation: Move to environment variable or feature flag for operational flexibility.

summary: FAIL — 2 blocking issues must be resolved before deploy. Both are code changes (PG version guard + cache invalidation), not process issues. Fix these, re-run PRR on the updated branch, and expect a PASS.
